memoryAudit.method: same-session baseline heap usage -> 3 scroll/nav/wait cycles -> forced Runtime.gc/HeapProfiler.collectGarbage after each cycle -> final heap snapshot summary

principles.summary: Respects prefers-color-scheme (light/dark variables), prefers-reduced-motion (transitions disabled), and forced-colors (borders preserved). All three preference signals handled.
Has transitions and respects prefers-reduced-motion (animation/transition duration reduced to ~0 under reduced-motion). View Transitions API available.

The visible dashboard has a clear title, summary metrics, an About link, and clickable principle rows that explain what each principle tests. It is a single-page dashboard, so breadcrumbs are not required.
The screenshot shows a focused data dashboard with summary cards and principle bars; no ads, popups, newsletter modals, or distracting promotional chrome are visible.
Layout evidence reports a viewport meta tag and no horizontal overflow; the dashboard content fits the captured viewport width.
The core task is browsing audit results, not completing a linear checkout/sign-up flow. The screenshot shows key aggregate results, principle issue rates, and affordances to learn more.
Trace summary is strong: FCP/LCP about 628ms, load event about 1.0s, no long tasks, and total blocking time 0ms. Coordinator evidence reports CLS approximately 0.
Known accessibility probes found lang=en, a good title, all buttons named, 35 buttons, 5 links, and no image alt issues.
Valid doctype and charset, no console errors, valid HTML structure, meta description present, correctly sized assets.
The page has a good title and raw H1, but non-JavaScript/raw content coverage is low and no meta description is present.

findings.title: Low non-JS content coverage
findings.evidence: discoverability.json reports coveragePct=11, rendered textChars=14010, raw textChars=995, and metaDescriptionPresentInRaw=null.
findings.suggestedFix: Server-render or statically include the key dashboard summary, principle names, and current counts so crawlers can understand the page without JavaScript.

findings.title: Missing meta description
findings.evidence: discoverability.json reports no raw meta description.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a descriptive meta description for search previews and crawlers.

principles.summary: The site is served over HTTPS on GitHub Pages and the screenshot shows no cookie consent wall, tracking prompt, account nag, or third-party ad surface. A deeper network/privacy audit was not provided for this specific pass.
The page is not an empty JS shell, but most rendered dashboard content is not present in the raw HTML, which weakens resilience for no-JavaScript/crawler contexts.

findings.title: Dashboard content depends heavily on client rendering
findings.evidence: discoverability.json reports contentVisibleWithoutJs=11 and coveragePct=11, while rendered text is much larger than raw HTML text.
findings.suggestedFix: Pre-render the critical dashboard data or provide a static fallback table/summary so the page remains useful when JavaScript fails.

principles.summary: The document language is set to lang=en. No deeper locale, timezone, or translation flow was tested.

The screenshot shows a transparent data dashboard with source/methodology-oriented copy and an About link. No visible dark patterns, consent walls, disguised ads, fake urgency, or nag banners are present.
The visible page is a text/data-heavy dashboard with no video, ad wall, or large media carousel. Trace timings are low; no detailed transfer-size evidence was supplied for this specific pass.
AI/crawler-readable raw content is limited compared with the rendered dashboard.

findings.title: Low raw content coverage for agents
findings.evidence: discoverability.json reports coveragePct=11 and raw contentTokens=68 versus rendered contentTokens=400.
findings.suggestedFix: Expose the key dashboard results, principle descriptions, and current counts in server-rendered/static HTML or linked JSON with clear metadata.

principles.summary: Same-session memory pass retained 0.0 MB after interaction cycles and forced GC; no monotonic leak signal above threshold.

verdict: State of the Web dashboard: 3 principles with issues after dog-food fixes (dark mode + reduced-motion + meta description added). Now respects all preference signals it audits others for.
_caveats: Judgement uses existing screenshot and supplied evidence only; no new browser capture or interaction flow was run.
Memory efficiency remains not-applicable until a rigorous heap-diff audit is run.
Memory was rechecked with same-session baseline, 3 interaction cycles, forced GC, and final heap snapshot; still a homepage smoke test, not full memlab trace attribution.
